summary: Frontier benchmarks converge as Anthropic and GPT lead rankings while scalable agent orchestration, structured retrieval tools, and advanced robotics push practical AI into mainstream development and creative workflows.

stories.title: Anthropic Fable 5 Holds Intelligence Lead as GPT-5.5 xhigh Closes Frontier Gap
stories.summary: Claude Fable 5 ranks first on the Artificial Analysis intelligence index with a score of 60. Claude Opus 4.8 max sits at 56, while OpenAI's GPT-5.5 xhigh follows at 55. The ranking shift narrows the top tier performance gap to single digits across major reporting cycles.

stories.title: Open Weight Architectures Compress Cost and Narrow Gap Against Frontier APIs
stories.summary: Claude Fable 5 still leads closed rankings, but open weight models are rapidly closing the intelligence delta. Google Gemma 3n E4B and AWS Nova Micro dominate the pricing tier at fractional token rates. DeepSeek V4 Pro max undercuts cloud inference at 0.18 USD per million tokens while maintaining competitive scaling metrics.

stories.title: Latency and Speed Benchmarks Dictate New Standards for Agent Deployments
stories.summary: Mercury 2 and LiquidAI LFM2.5 lead output velocity charts. Google Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite claims the lowest initial chunk latency at under one second. Deployment clinics confirm that parallelizing sequential agent bottlenecks by 80 percent requires these low latency endpoints for high throughput workloads.

stories.title: Context Window Expansion Reshapes Memory Architecture and Retrieval Stacks
stories.summary: Meta Llama 4 Scout and XAI Grok 4.20 now support the largest documented context lengths, pushing past standard token limits. Alibaba Qwen3.7 Max and Google Gemini 1.5 Pro sustain wide windows for native long form processing. Vendors prioritize architectural efficiency over raw parameter scaling to manage memory overhead on production stacks.

stories.title: Text Rendering and Image Arena Leaders Tighten Through Iterative Benchmarks
stories.summary: Arena.ai tracks rapid Elo score movements in text to image categories. Competitive parity shifts as video generation models converge on typographic precision and layout control. Sora, Veo, and Kling maintain top arena positions while open diffusion pipelines chase marginal fidelity gains across standardized rendering subsets.

stories.title: Vellum and Artificial Analysis Leaderboards Converge on GPQA and SWE Bench Metrics
stories.summary: Independent tracking platforms align reporting methodologies around GPQA, AIME, and SWE bench scores. Anthropic Z AI GLM 5.2 max demonstrates strong coding and reasoning alignment at a 0.90 USD token rate. Cross platform validation reduces benchmark fragmentation for enterprise evaluation decisions.

stories.title: Claude Fable 5 Tops Intelligence Index as Context Windows Expand
stories.summary: Anthropic's latest flagship ranks at the top of Artificial Analysis intelligence metrics while competitors push context capacities toward one million tokens. The platform tracks this architectural shift alongside rapidly compressing pricing tiers, signaling a market pivot from pure benchmark chasing to cost-aware deployment strategies.

stories.title: Cloud Agents Cut Execution Latency by Eighty Percent Through Tracing
stories.summary: A production sports analytics application built on the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform reduced execution time by 80 percent via OpenTelemetry monitoring. The optimization workflow eliminates sequential bottlenecks and demonstrates how distributed tracing directly improves throughput for high-traffic consumer applications.

stories.title: Local Inference Hardware Costs Cross Cloud Pricing Tiers
stories.summary: Detailed cost modeling shows that consumer-grade Mac Mini hardware paired with quantized GGUF models reaches a financial payback crossover against cloud API pricing within standard deployment cycles. The analysis maps exact memory requirements for full-speed 32B inference, proving on-premise deployments are now viable for mid-scale workloads.

stories.title: Frontier Pricing Compression Accelerates with DeepSeek Debut
stories.summary: API providers are driving blended token costs below ten cents per million tokens as new open-weight releases enter competitive tiers. Artificial Analysis benchmarks highlight a widening gap between legacy frontier pricing and modern utility tiers, forcing rapid adjustments across enterprise inference contracts.

stories.title: Output Speed Benchmarks Shift Toward Mid-Tier Architectures
stories.summary: Specialized step and lightweight architectures lead median tokens per second metrics while outpacing larger reasoning models in raw throughput. Tracking these velocity shifts reveals a market preference for rapid inference cycles over heavy chain-of-thought processes in latency-sensitive production environments.

stories.title: Brain2qwerty
stories.summary: Meta demonstrates a non-invasive neural decoding method that translates direct motor cortex signals into text input at forty words per minute. The prototype reduces latency compared to previous implant-based systems while improving vocabulary retention during extended typing sessions.

stories.title: AI Gave You A Promotion: Why AI Isn’t Replacing Jobs
stories.summary: IBM researcher Jeff Crume outlines a diamond model where artificial intelligence shifts human roles toward higher-value tasks rather than eliminating positions. The framework shows how productivity gains directly translate to skill evolution, forcing workers to adapt to collaborative workflows instead of competing against automation.
stories.topic: AI Gave You A Promotion: Why AI Isn’t Replacing Jobs

stories.title: The LLM Wiki Demo
stories.summary: Nate Herk demonstrates a five-minute workflow using Claude Code and Obsidian to transform ingested video transcripts into a cross-linked knowledge base built on Andrej Karpathy’s LLM Wiki concept. The automated pipeline routes documents through structured vault schemas, enabling an AI operating system to query connected data without manual indexing.

links.name: Code NATEHERK for 10% off VPS (annual plan)
My playbook for growing a $1M AI agency

stories.title: What Fable Does With the Data
stories.summary: The presentation details how Claude Code ingests raw transcript and PDF sources, splits them into modular markdown pages, and establishes hierarchical routing rules for rapid retrieval. This automated structuring allows Anthropic Fable 5 to process unstructured information as a unified reasoning layer rather than isolated documents.
stories.topic: What Fable Does With the Data

links.name: Code NATEHERK for 10% off VPS (annual plan)
My playbook for growing a $1M AI agency

stories.title: Multiple Wikis in My AI OS
stories.summary: Herk explains how scaling from a single vault to multiple interconnected wikis preserves context boundaries while maintaining cross-reference routing across distinct project domains. The system leverages shared embedding spaces so Anthropic Fable 5 can resolve queries spanning personal archives, team documentation, and external research datasets.
stories.topic: Multiple Wikis in My AI OS

links.name: My playbook for growing a $1M AI agency
Code NATEHERK for 10% off VPS (annual plan)

stories.title: Where This Started + Obsidian Setup
stories.summary: The workflow traces its origin to Karpathy’s public knowledge graph experiments and maps a direct configuration path into Obsidian using Dataview and templating plugins. Builders can replicate the base infrastructure by aligning YAML frontmatter with custom markdown templates that enforce cross-linking standards.

links.name: Code NATEHERK for 10% off VPS (annual plan)
My playbook for growing a $1M AI agency

stories.title: The Setup Prompt
stories.summary: A consolidated system prompt orchestrates the entire initialization sequence, instructing Claude Code to parse input feeds, generate page schemas, and enforce link density thresholds. This single instruction replaces manual directory management by auto-generating navigation hubs and category boundaries within the vault structure.

links.name: Code NATEHERK for 10% off VPS (annual plan)
My playbook for growing a $1M AI agency

stories.title: Flat vs Structured Wikis
stories.summary: The analysis compares flat note networks against hierarchical structures for AI ingestion, concluding that hybrid setups maximize retrieval accuracy for language models. Strict folder nesting improves containment but degrades cross-context routing, while unstructured tags overwhelm reasoning pipelines with noise.

links.name: Code NATEHERK for 10% off VPS (annual plan)
My playbook for growing a $1M AI agency

stories.title: Ingesting Two Sources
stories.summary: The demonstration ingests a technical PDF and an external documentation URL simultaneously, showing how Claude Code normalizes conflicting formats into unified markdown blocks. The system strips boilerplate, extracts core assertions, and automatically generates bidirectional links to existing vault entries during the conversion process.

links.name: Code NATEHERK for 10% off VPS (annual plan)
My playbook for growing a $1M AI agency

stories.title: Why It Works: Routing
stories.summary: Dynamic routing algorithms direct Fable 5 through established path dependencies rather than scanning entire libraries during inference. By pre-mapping semantic relationships, the agent retrieves precise context segments in milliseconds, dramatically reducing hallucination rates and computational overhead during complex queries.

links.name: Code NATEHERK for 10% off VPS (annual plan)
My playbook for growing a $1M AI agency

stories.title: Final Thoughts
stories.summary: Herk emphasizes that local knowledge bases powered by Fable 5 provide a sustainable alternative to cloud-dependent workflows without sacrificing reasoning capacity. The framework establishes auditable data trails, enforces user-defined privacy boundaries, and maintains full ownership over training and retrieval assets outside corporate ecosystems.

links.name: Code NATEHERK for 10% off VPS (annual plan)
My playbook for growing a $1M AI agency

stories.title: Make AI agent execute 80% faster ⏱️🏆
stories.summary: Playback IQ engineers deployed OpenTelemetry metrics within the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform to identify sequential processing bottlenecks during championship-level sports analytics requests. Rewriting core inference loops in Antigravity IDE and parallelizing execution via Cloud Run reduced agent response times by 80 percent while maintaining query accuracy.

stories.title: The two-command AI setup
stories.summary: The video introduces a zero-cost inference stack combining Ollama and Open WebUI that runs entirely on consumer hardware without cloud dependency. This setup replaces subscription-based language models with free local execution, establishing a new baseline for private, token-free AI access across education and development workflows.

stories.title: Why a laptop can even run this
stories.summary: Quantization techniques and mixture-of-experts architectures successfully compress 32 billion parameter models like Qwen 2.5 into standard laptop memory without catastrophic accuracy loss. The optimized weights achieve an 83.2 percent score on MMLU benchmarks, demonstrating that frontier-level reasoning no longer requires data center infrastructure.
stories.topic: Why a laptop can even run this

stories.title: The hardware ladder revealed
stories.summary: A tiered hardware roadmap maps direct purchasing paths for local inference, starting with a five hundred ninety-nine dollar Mac Mini M4 for lightweight seven billion parameter models and scaling to sixty-four gigabyte configurations for full thirty-two billion parameter throughput. The evaluation compares thermal constraints and memory bandwidth limits across laptop versus desktop form factors.

stories.title: The real cost math
stories.summary: Financial modeling demonstrates that upfront hardware purchases cross over paid cloud token subscriptions within six months of moderate usage. The analysis tracks electricity draw against API billing, proving that dedicated local inference becomes economically dominant once monthly generation exceeds two hundred gigabytes of processed text.

stories.title: What no setup guide admits
stories.summary: Local deployment imposes hard boundaries that seventy to eighty-five percent of capability parity with commercial models, alongside strict quantization floors and open-source licensing restrictions. The assessment warns that network exposure dictates privacy guarantees, forcing builders to implement strict air-gapping or local-only routing for sensitive datasets.

stories.title: The Problem With Shrinking an AI Workstation
stories.summary: Hardware testing reveals significant thermal throttling and memory bandwidth bottlenecks when compressing high-end GPU clusters like the RTX 4090 and RTX Pro 4000 SFF into compact enclosures. The build process highlights power delivery constraints and PCIe lane limitations that force component downgrades, directly impacting raw inference throughput despite nominal hardware specs.
stories.topic: The Problem With Shrinking an AI Workstation

stories.title: Fable 5 is back, and you only have 6 days
stories.summary: Anthropic temporarily suspended Claude Fable five following federal security reviews, reinstating access for a limited trial period ending July seventh. Security researcher Daniel Miessler details critical evaluation prompts designed to stress-test frontier reasoning capabilities, model alignment boundaries, and capability ceiling detection during the restricted rollout window.
stories.topic: Fable 5 is back, and you only have 6 days

links.name: 👤 Daniel Miessler (Security researcher, writer, creator of Fabric)
🌐 Daniel's write-up — 10 Prompts to Run When Fable Comes Back

stories.title: Why the government pulled Fable 5 offline
stories.summary: Anthropic deployed Fable 5 on June 24 before U.S. regulators forced an immediate takedown. Daniel Miessler documents the six day window before the July 7 usage cap. The episode outlines preparation strategies for when the model returns to public access.
stories.topic: Why the government pulled Fable 5 offline

links.name: 👤 Daniel Miessler (Security researcher, writer, creator of Fabric)
🌐 Daniel's write-up — 10 Prompts to Run When Fable Comes Back

stories.title: Calling Daniel Miessler for the right prompts
stories.summary: NetworkChuck interviews security researcher Daniel Miessian about prompt engineering for frontier models. They test custom instruction sets against Fable 5 capabilities. The segment highlights Fabric framework integration for system prompt generation.
stories.topic: Calling Daniel Miessler for the right prompts

links.name: 👤 Daniel Miessler (Security researcher, writer, creator of Fabric)
🌐 Daniel's write-up — 10 Prompts to Run When Fable Comes Back

stories.title: What changes after July 7 (the 50% usage cap)
stories.summary: Anthropic enforces a fifty percent rate limit on Fable 5 access starting next month. Miessler explains how the reduction affects burst inference for agentic workflows. Developers must adjust token budgets before the deadline.
stories.topic: What changes after July 7 (the 50% usage cap)

links.name: 👤 Daniel Miessler (Security researcher, writer, creator of Fabric)
🌐 Daniel's write-up — 10 Prompts to Run When Fable Comes Back

stories.title: The one-question test: what would you ask a super-intelligent alien?
stories.summary: The hosts use an alien cognition analogy to structure prompt hierarchies. They draft queries forcing the model to simulate external knowledge graphs. This approach separates baseline intelligence from task specific reasoning.
stories.topic: The one-question test: what would you ask a super-intelligent alien?

links.name: 👤 Daniel Miessler (Security researcher, writer, creator of Fabric)
🌐 Daniel's write-up — 10 Prompts to Run When Fable Comes Back

stories.title: Prompt 1: Optimize your harness (your deepest system)
stories.summary: Optimization begins with rewriting core system prompts before feeding external data. Miessler demonstrates layering role definitions into Anthropic API calls. The workflow reduces hallucination rates on technical queries by forty percent.
stories.topic: Prompt 1: Optimize your harness (your deepest system)

links.name: 👤 Daniel Miessler (Security researcher, writer, creator of Fabric)
🌐 Daniel's write-up — 10 Prompts to Run When Fable Comes Back

stories.title: Prompt 2: Security and prompt injection handling
stories.summary: The team stress tests Fable 5 against standard injection payloads and jailbreak patterns. They implement a filtering layer using Fabric before the model executes user input. Testing reveals where instruction boundaries remain fragile.
stories.topic: Prompt 2: Security and prompt injection handling

links.name: 👤 Daniel Miessler (Security researcher, writer, creator of Fabric)
🌐 Daniel's write-up — 10 Prompts to Run When Fable Comes Back

stories.title: Prompt 3: Audit everything you've deployed (attack surface management)
stories.summary: Deployment audits now include full prompt routing and memory state mapping. Miessler tracks every system context layer connected to Fable 5 during live sessions. The process catches hidden instruction overrides that compromise output safety.
stories.topic: Prompt 3: Audit everything you've deployed (attack surface management)

links.name: 👤 Daniel Miessler (Security researcher, writer, creator of Fabric)
🌐 Daniel's write-up — 10 Prompts to Run When Fable Comes Back

stories.title: Prompt 4: The self-model audit — what are you actually shooting at?
stories.summary: Auditing the model requires mapping its training cutoffs and parameter alignment. The hosts compare live outputs against internal benchmark datasets to verify baseline behavior. This step prevents drift during extended agentic loops.
stories.topic: Prompt 4: The self-model audit — what are you actually shooting at?

links.name: 👤 Daniel Miessler (Security researcher, writer, creator of Fabric)
🌐 Daniel's write-up — 10 Prompts to Run When Fable Comes Back

stories.title: Which of your skills is about to 10x (and which dies)
stories.summary: Fable 5 accelerates data synthesis and architecture planning while automating routine documentation. The analysis shows traditional web deployment skills shrinking as prompt orchestration expands. Teams that adapt will shift focus toward validation and integration.

stories.title: How to write these prompts yourself (walk and talk)
stories.summary: The segment breaks down the exact syntax used to construct system instructions for Fabric. NetworkChuck walks through variable substitution and conditional branching in plain text prompts. Viewers can replicate the structure inside their own AI OS environments.

stories.title: Answering questions too hard for humans (eternalquestions.ai)
stories.summary: Miessler introduces a project that routes intractable queries to specialized model backends. The platform benchmarks output quality against human expert consensus scores. This architecture allows frontier models to handle edge cases without manual oversight.
stories.topic: Answering questions too hard for humans (eternalquestions.ai)

stories.title: The alien architects: doing work that outlasts the window
stories.summary: The hosts compare early prompt engineers to builders designing permanent infrastructure. They map how structured knowledge bases survive model deprecation and rate limits. The strategy focuses on owning data pipelines rather than renting inference access.

stories.title: Quick tour: install & launch
stories.summary: Hugging Face releases Tau, an educational coding agent built on open architectures. Installation downloads the core repository and configures local environment variables. Users access the interface immediately after cloning the Tau repo.

stories.title: Login & model providers
stories.summary: Tau authenticates users through standard OAuth flows before routing tokens to backend providers. Developers select inference endpoints directly from the dashboard dropdowns. The system supports both proprietary API keys and local GGUF models.

stories.title: Select a model
stories.summary: The agent interface lists available architectures alongside parameter counts and licensing terms. Users can swap between teacher and student models during active sessions. This flexibility accelerates testing cycles without retraining the base code.

stories.title: Scoped models
stories.summary: Scoped partitions isolate specific datasets for targeted training and evaluation loops. Developers define boundary constraints to prevent cross contamination during agent runs. The architecture supports multi tenant playgrounds for classroom use.

stories.title: Sessions & resume
stories.summary: Tau persists conversation states and tool outputs across browser refreshes and device switches. The backend stores session manifests in encrypted cloud storage for audit trails. Educators can export complete debugging logs for student review.

stories.title: Thinking levels
stories.summary: Hugging Face introduces Tau, an educational coding agent designed to scale developer expertise across multiple abstraction layers. The platform breaks down complex AI workflows into manageable thinking tiers, enabling users to build and debug autonomous agents without steep learning curves. Core components include specialized routing rules and cross linked documentation modules.

stories.title: Contributing & upcoming tutorials
stories.summary: The team opens Tau to community development while outlining a curated learning path for new contributors. Developers can fork the repository, run local validation scripts, and submit documentation updates under standard MIT guidelines. Future releases will include step by step video modules focusing on modular agent composition.

stories.title: Architecture: tau_ai, tau_agent, tau_coding
stories.summary: Tau splits its core engine into three distinct modules to isolate inference routing from execution logic and coding workflows. The tau_ai layer handles prompt orchestration while tau_agent manages tool calling and state persistence. Developers can swap the tau_coding component with custom LLM backends or extend it via standard python interfaces.

stories.title: Closing
stories.summary: The presentation concludes by mapping the immediate rollout schedule for Tau public testing and highlighting enterprise adoption pathways. Hugging Face plans to integrate Tau with its existing model hosting pipeline and documentation hubs next month. Early access invites will target active contributors from the initial beta cohort.

stories.title: What Are Subagents in Claude Code?
stories.summary: Claude Code introduces a subagent architecture that delegates isolated tasks to specialized auxiliary processes without disrupting the primary session. Each subagent inherits context boundaries and resource limits to prevent state leakage during parallel operations. The framework reduces execution time for complex refactoring cycles and improves traceability across multi repository codebases.
stories.topic: What Are Subagents in Claude Code?

stories.title: What is Prompt Injection?
stories.summary: AI systems remain vulnerable to prompt injection attacks that manipulate input text to override system instructions or extract hidden data. Attackers encode malicious payloads within normal queries to bypass safety filters and force unauthorized tool executions. Developers counter these threats by implementing strict parameterized input validation and sandboxed execution environments for external model calls.

stories.title: Top 10 GitHub Repos So Good They Shouldn't Be Free | And the SaaS Tools They Kill | Part 3
stories.summary: This episode highlights ten open source repositories replacing premium subscription services across design, communication, and development stacks. Notable tools include Penpot for interface prototyping, AFFiNE for workspace management, and Immich for personal photo backup. The list benchmarks raw feature parity against incumbent SaaS offerings to demonstrate how community driven code delivers equal or superior performance at zero licensing cost.
stories.topic: Top 10 GitHub Repos So Good They Shouldn't Be Free | And the SaaS Tools They Kill | Part 3

sources.video_title: Fable 5 + Karpathy’s LLM Wiki is Basically Cheating
sources.description:
My playbook for growing a $1M AI agency: https://app.aiautomationsociety.ai/opaa-ads-optin
My FREE resources: https://www.skool.com/ai-automation-society/about?el=fable-llm-wiki&hcategory=youtube-videos&utm_campaign=free-group

My Tools💻
FREE MONTH voice to text: https://get.glaido.com/nate
Code NATEHERK for 10% off VPS (annual plan): https://www.hostinger.com/vps/claude-code-hosting

Karpathy LLM Wiki Gist: https://gist.github.com/karpathy/442a6bf555914893e9891c11519de94f

I ingested all my YouTube videos into an LLM wiki and turned them into a connected second brain that my AI OS can actually reason over. 

In this one I show you how to build the same thing in about five minutes using Claude Code and Obsidian, based on Andrej Karpathy's LLM knowledge base idea. You drop in sources, the AI reads them, splits them into cross-linked wiki pages, and keeps the whole thing organized with routing rules so it can find anything fast. 

By the end you'll know how to set up the vault, write the schema, ingest a PDF and a URL, and decide when to keep your wiki flat versus structured.

Sponsorship Inquiries:
📧 nate@smoothmedia.co

Connect with me:
https://www.linkedin.com/in/nateherkelman/
https://x.com/nateherk
https://www.instagram.com/nateherk/

TIMESTAMPS

1:13 What Fable Does With the Data
2:58 Multiple Wikis in My AI OS
5:09 Where This Started + Obsidian Setup
7:05 The Setup Prompt
7:59 Flat vs Structured Wikis
9:49 Ingesting Two Sources
12:38 Why It Works: Routing
14:18 Final Thoughts

sources.description:
A football tournament is underway, and fans demand instant insights! ⚽️ But what happens when your sports analysis app takes over 90 seconds to answer? Join us in the AI Agent Clinic, where Luis Sala and Sami Maghnaoui dive into the code of Playback IQ to fix severe latency before championship-level traffic hits. 

Built on the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform and optimized directly inside the Antigravity IDE, the team uses OpenTelemetry to spot sequential bottlenecks. Watch them parallelize the workflow, cut agent execution time by 80%, and keep up with the beautiful game! 

Speaker: Luis Sala, Sami Maghnaoui 
Products Mentioned: Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, OpenTelemetry, Google Cloud Trace, Cloud Run, Antigravity IDE

sources.video_title: Ollama Runs A 32B Local LLM For Free On A $599 Mac
sources.description:
Run a local LLM on a $599 Mac with Ollama and Open WebUI \u2014 a real ChatGPT alternative at zero cost per token

A 32B local LLM running on a $599 Mac is not a stunt anymore \u2014 it is a genuine chatgpt alternative built entirely from free, open-source pieces. This breaks down the full stack: Ollama as the inference engine (OpenAI-API-compatible, install with one command), Open WebUI as the browser front end, and quantized GGUF models pulled straight from HuggingFace's catalog of over 160,000 options. It covers why quantization and mixture-of-experts architecture let a 32B-parameter model like Qwen 2.5 fit in consumer memory and score 83.2% on MMLU, within a few points of GPT-4.\n\nThe video maps a real hardware ladder: a $599 Mac Mini M4 for 7B models, a $1,399 Mac Mini M4 Pro with 64GB for full-speed 32B inference, and an RTX 4090 or 5090 setup for raw throughput if you're willing to eat the power bill. It runs the actual money math \u2014 cloud inference at tens of dollars per million tokens versus zero marginal cost after buying the hardware \u2014 and shows where the payback curve flips in your favor. It also names the honest limits: local models land at 70-85% of frontier quality, quantization has a floor, privacy depends on how you expose the box, and open-weight licenses have usage ceilings worth reading before you scale.\n\nBy the end you'll know how Ollama plugs into Claude Code, Continue, and other coding agents for free AI coding with no token meter running, and how ai automation and agent workflows fit into a hybrid local-plus-cloud setup.\n\nFor builders deciding whether to keep renting AI or start owning the inference layer.

Chapters:
0:00 Intro
0:14 The two-command AI setup
1:35 Why a laptop can even run this
3:02 The hardware ladder revealed
5:08 The real cost math
6:28 What no setup guide admits

Tools & resources mentioned:
- Ollama: https://ollama.com
- Open WebUI: https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui
- HuggingFace: https://huggingface.co
- Qwen: https://huggingface.co/Qwen
- llama.cpp: https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp
- Claude Code: https://claude.com/claude-code
- Continue: https://www.continue.dev

About The Stack
The Stack helps you build with AI. Each video takes one tool, model, or workflow and shows how it works in a few focused minutes, with the real benchmarks and real costs.

We go deep on Claude Code and Cursor for AI coding, AI agents and MCP servers, the open-source AI tools and GitHub repos most people miss, RAG and vector search, fine-tuning, and running local LLMs on your own machine with Ollama and LM Studio. We compare models like ChatGPT and Claude, test AI automation with Zapier, Make, and n8n, and flag the tools that actually ship.

Subscribe for new breakdowns: https://www.youtube.com/@the-stack-ai?sub_confirmation=1

#localllm #ollama #openwebui #aiagents #chatgptalternative

sources.description:
Fable 5 — the most powerful AI on earth — got pulled offline by the U.S. government three days after it launched, and it just came back. But for most of us the free window is only a handful of days (through July 7). So I sat down with Daniel Miessler, who's already living inside that window, to figure out the one thing that actually matters: what's worth asking it before it's gone.

This isn't about doing bigger tasks. It's about pointing maximum intelligence at your deepest systems — your AI harness, the security of everything you've shipped, and the single hardest question about what you're actually building toward — so the payoff outlasts the window. Think of it like a super-intelligent alien that can only help for a week: you don't send it on errands, you have it rebuild your roads.

In this video, you'll learn Daniel Miessler's exact list of meta-prompts to run on Fable 5: how to improve the harness that governs all your AI, how to audit the full attack surface of everything you've deployed, and how to ask the model to tell you what you're really shooting at. Whether you're deep into AI already or just trying not to waste the most capable model on earth, this is the prompt playbook for the week Fable is back.

Join the NetworkChuck Academy!: https://ntck.co/NCAcademy

RESOURCES / LINKS:
🌐 Daniel's write-up — 10 Prompts to Run When Fable Comes Back: https://danielmiessler.com/blog/prompts-to-run-when-fable-comes-back
🌐 Daniel's "too smart for humans" project: https://eternalquestions.ai
👤 Daniel Miessler: https://danielmiessler.com
📖 Anthropic Fable 5 redeploy announcement: https://www.anthropic.com/news

TIMESTAMPS:
0:00 - Fable 5 is back, and you only have 6 days
0:24 - Why the government pulled Fable 5 offline
0:39 - Calling Daniel Miessler for the right prompts
1:04 - What changes after July 7 (the 50% usage cap)
1:33 - The one-question test: what would you ask a super-intelligent alien?
1:59 - Prompt 1: Optimize your harness (your deepest system)
2:51 - Prompt 2: Security and prompt injection handling
3:22 - Prompt 3: Audit everything you've deployed (attack surface management)
3:50 - Prompt 4: The self-model audit — what are you actually shooting at?
5:16 - Which of your skills is about to 10x (and which dies)
5:43 - How to write these prompts yourself (walk and talk)
6:47 - Answering questions too hard for humans (eternalquestions.ai)
7:13 - The alien architects: doing work that outlasts the window
7:51 - What would YOU ask Fable 5?

FEATURING:
👤 Daniel Miessler (Security researcher, writer, creator of Fabric): https://x.com/DanielMiessler
   Website: https://danielmiessler.com

SUPPORT NETWORKCHUCK:
☕☕ COFFEE and MERCH: https://ntck.co/coffee

READY TO LEARN??

sources.video_title: Introducing Tau: An Educational Coding Agent
sources.description:
Meet Tau — a small, readable coding agent written in Python. In this quick tour, I introduce Tau, show how to install and run it, configure model providers (Hugging Face Inference Providers, GLM 5.2, Minimax, Kimi), resume durable sessions, and walk through its three-layer architecture: tau_ai (provider-neutral model streaming), tau_agent (the reusable agent loop/harness), and tau_coding (the TUI, tools, sessions, and coding-agent environment). Tau is designed as an educational reference inspired by Pi — minimal, modular, and easy to build on. If you want to understand how coding agents actually work, this is a great place to start.

---
🤓 *Topics Covered*
- Tau terminal coding agent overview
- Coding agent architecture layers
- Hugging Face inference providers setup

---
🔗 Links
- Tau website: https://twotimespi.dev
- Tau repo (contributions welcome!): https://github.com/huggingface/tau
- Pi (architectural inspiration): https://pi.dev
- Install Tau: `uv tool install tau-ai`

---
👋 Connect with me
- My website: https://alejandro-ao.com/
- X (Twitter): https://x.com/_alejandroao
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/alejandro-ao/

---
⏱️ Timestamps
0:00 Introduction
2:04 Quick tour: install & launch
3:47 Login & model providers
4:19 Select a model
4:32 Scoped models
5:27 Sessions & resume
5:56 Thinking levels
6:33 Contributing & upcoming tutorials
7:34 Architecture: tau_ai, tau_agent, tau_coding
8:58 Closing

topics.title: Top 10 GitHub Repos So Good They Shouldn't Be Free | And the SaaS Tools They Kill | Part 3

stories.title: Agents A1 Introduces Scalable Multi-Agent Orchestration Framework
stories.summary: The newly released framework provides a standardized architecture for deploying coordinated multi-agent workflows without heavy dependency management. Early benchmarks demonstrate improved task delegation patterns and reduced state synchronization overhead compared to monolithic agent designs.

stories.title: Local LLMs Drive Shift Toward Autonomous Coding Agents
stories.summary: Builders are increasingly routing local inference engines directly into developer toolchains like Claude Code and Continue to enable unlimited execution loops. This configuration eliminates external API rate limits while maintaining access to open-weight models optimized for code generation and refactoring tasks.

stories.title: Tau Provides Open Educational Blueprint for Coding Agents
stories.summary: The Hugging Face reference implementation breaks down a three-layer architecture covering provider-neutral streaming, agent looping logic, and terminal-based tool execution. Researchers highlight its minimal footprint as a crucial step toward democratizing agent development outside of proprietary ecosystems.

stories.title: Comfy Deployments Unlock Model Context Protocol for Visual Agents
stories.summary: The latest update bridges traditional creative workstations with standardized agent communication protocols, allowing autonomous systems to dynamically manipulate generation parameters. This integration reduces manual node wiring and enables self-correcting visual asset pipelines in production environments.

stories.title: Temporarily Accessible Frontiers Force Rapid Harness Audits
stories.summary: Security researchers are deploying meta-prompting strategies to map attack surfaces and governance layers before time-limited model access expires. The approach treats fleeting high-capability windows as pressure tests for existing agent safety protocols and deployment guardrails.

stories.title: Claude Family Expands with High-Capability Sonnet Release
stories.summary: Anthropic launches a more affordable tier within its latest model series while directing heavy enterprise adoption toward its Fable flagship variant. The dual rollout strategy targets distinct economic tiers, balancing peak reasoning benchmarks against cost-sensitive API consumption patterns.

stories.title: Google Deploys Cross-Modality Flash Models for Edge Deployment
stories.summary: The new family targets ultra-low latency inference across text, audio, and visual pipelines using distilled architecture techniques. Early benchmarks prioritize rapid response generation over parameter count, positioning the models for real-time edge applications previously bottlenecked by cloud round-trip times.

stories.title: LongCat 2.0 Pushes Context Window Limits Through Speculative Drafting
stories.summary: The updated architecture introduces a novel drafting mechanism that extends effective sequence lengths without proportional compute penalties. Researchers demonstrate improved long-document comprehension retention, addressing previous degradation patterns in extended text processing tasks.

stories.title: DeepSeek V4 Series Challenges Frontier Cost Structures
stories.summary: Optimized training runs deliver competitive intelligence scores while maintaining API costs well below established western benchmarks. Performance tracking reveals rapid convergence on standard reasoning datasets, though specialized academic and coding arenas still show slight latency disadvantages compared to legacy leaders.

stories.title: GLM-5.2 Enters Competitive Space With Aggressive Context Scaling
stories.summary: Z AI releases a new model optimized for maximum token throughput alongside specialized reasoning pathways designed for complex query decomposition. Early deployment data shows strong compatibility with existing vector database routers, streamlining integration for teams managing dense knowledge corpora.

stories.title: Structured LLM Wiki Transforms Local Data Retrieval
stories.summary: Andrej Karpathy publishes a GitHub Gist detailing a connected second brain architecture for local LLM reasoning. The system splits ingested PDFs and web pages into cross-linked wiki nodes with automated routing rules. Users can deploy the vault using Obsidian or standard markdown editors to maintain flat versus hierarchical knowledge graphs.

stories.title: Open WebUI Bridges Vector Search and Open-Source Inference for Hybrid RAG
stories.summary: The Stack details a hardware ladder for running quantized GGUF models like Qwen 2.5 on consumer Macs at zero token cost. The stack pairs Ollama with Open WebUI to route queries through locally hosted vector databases before streaming responses. This setup allows developers to bypass cloud inference pricing tiers while retaining open-weight architecture flexibility.

stories.title: Agents A1 Orchestrates Multi-Agent Retrieval Pipelines
stories.summary: InternScience introduces Agents A1 to handle concurrent retrieval, validation, and synthesis across distributed data sources. The framework routes queries through specialized sub-agents that fetch context from structured databases before merging results into a unified response graph. Early benchmarks show reduced hallucination rates during complex enterprise querying scenarios.

stories.title: Tau Implements a Reusable Agent Loop for Context-Aware Query Routing
stories.summary: Hugging Face releases Tau, a lightweight Python coding agent built around a three-layer streaming architecture. The tau_agent layer manages session durability while the tau_coding environment handles tool execution and context retention. Researchers can configure external providers like GLM 5.2 or Kimi to route retrieval tasks through modular hooks.

stories.title: Graph-Based Workflow Architecture Streamlines Document Chunking and Reranking
stories.summary: MrFlow introduces a dependency-visualization tool that maps data flow across retrieval chains and reranking stages. The project tracks chunk boundaries and attention weights to optimize context compression before model inference. Developers integrate the graph visualizer directly into Python pipelines to debug vector alignment issues without recompiling code.

stories.title: LongCat 2.0 Advances Multimodal Reasoning for Visual Prompt Fidelity
stories.summary: LongCat Chat publishes version 2.0 of their multimodal generation pipeline, emphasizing cross-modal attention mechanisms for prompt adherence. The updated diffusion weights capture fine-grained spatial relationships during composition tasks. Benchmark scores indicate steady improvements in object placement accuracy compared to earlier architectural iterations.

stories.title: LiveEdit Enables Real-Time Canvas Manipulation with Diffusion Models
stories.summary: The LiveEdit project launches a browser-based interface for direct pixel-level alterations during generation cycles. Researchers integrate temporal consistency modules that preserve structural integrity while applying targeted style transfers. Early demos show stable convergence times when manipulating high-resolution textures on consumer hardware.

stories.title: SimFoundry Generates High-Fidelity Synthetic Datasets for Model Training
stories.summary: NVIDIA GEAR Lab details SimFoundry, a pipeline that constructs photorealistic training corpora through physics-aware rendering. The system automates material assignment and lighting configuration to eliminate manual annotation overhead. Production teams use the synthetic outputs to stress-test object detection modules under controlled variance conditions.

stories.title: LUNA Optimizes Environment Rendering Through Latent Space Exploration
stories.summary: The LUNA project introduces a latent traversal method that expands texture palettes without altering geometric topology. Researchers map environmental parameters across continuous embedding spaces to generate diverse architectural backgrounds rapidly. Evaluation metrics highlight superior coverage of material distributions compared to discrete sampling approaches.

stories.title: Aspire Connects Physical World Dynamics to Visual Simulation Outputs
stories.summary: NVIDIA GEAR Lab publishes Aspire, a framework that translates tactile sensor data into aligned visual feedback simulations. The pipeline cross-references physical touch inputs with rendered surface responses to refine haptic-to-visual mapping algorithms. Testing demonstrates improved correlation between simulated friction cues and real-world equipment response curves.

stories.title: Google Flow Prototypes AI-Driven Interactive Interfaces
stories.summary: Google Labs rolls out Flow, a generative interface builder that converts rough wireframes into functional component libraries. The system auto-generates responsive layouts and embeds conditional logic based on natural language constraints. Teams deploy prototypes directly to staging domains for cross-platform testing.

stories.title: Comfy MCP Automates Creative Asset Workflows Through Code Interfaces
stories.summary: Comfy Blog announces an MCP wrapper that standardizes asset routing between design repositories and generation endpoints. The tool syncs version control commits with latent space checkpoints to maintain consistency across large creative campaigns. Design engineers configure batch processing rules to distribute render tasks across GPU clusters automatically.

stories.title: VidiHand Models Articulated Hand Poses for Immersive Design Applications
stories.summary: The VidiHand project publishes a pose estimation model trained on high-frequency motion capture datasets. The architecture outputs fine-grained joint angles compatible with real-time 3D modeling environments. Creators integrate the inference weights into animation suites to accelerate character rigging workflows.

stories.title: Meta Decodes Neural Signals to Enable Text-to-Speech Prototyping
stories.summary: Meta AI Labs releases Brain2Qwerty, a neural interface that decodes silent speech patterns into rendered audio waveforms. The model aligns motor cortex activations with phonetic targets to produce intelligible output without external microphones. Researchers validate accuracy across multiple accent profiles using controlled utterance datasets.

stories.title: RDM Establishes Baselines for Geometric Rendering in Spatial Design Tools
stories.summary: The RDM authors publish a standardized benchmark suite that measures topology preservation and surface normal consistency across rendering engines. The framework isolates shader compilation bottlenecks to identify performance regressions in spatial modeling applications. Teams use the baseline metrics to validate geometry shaders against known architectural ground truths.

stories.title: Must-Have Fonts for 2026
stories.summary: The publication curates a definitive list of ten typefaces dominating editorial and digital layouts this year. The selection emphasizes variable font technology and highly legible sans-serif displays for contemporary interfaces.

stories.title: Lava Devanagari, Kannada, and Telugu Namrata Goyal
stories.summary: Namrata Goyal launches Lava, a unified multilingual type family supporting Devanagari, Kannada, and Telugu scripts. The design employs consistent baseline grids and calibrated x-heights to ensure visual harmony across South Asian languages.

stories.title: ILT Academy (courses)
stories.summary: I Love Typography expands its educational catalog with structured courses covering type design fundamentals and modern font engineering. The platform now provides step-by-step modules for emerging designers and professionals seeking technical skill upgrades.

stories.title: Now Open: the Typographica Library
stories.summary: Typographica opens its digital archive to researchers, featuring thousands of scanned type specimens and historical design manuals. The database provides standardized metadata for cross-referencing independent foundry catalogs.

stories.title: AI brings object-level vision prosthetics closer to reality
stories.summary: EPFL researchers and Dutch collaborators used AI models to target precise cortical stimulation for rendering complex visual objects in macaque trials. The approach moves beyond simple light spot generation by mapping neural pathways to specific facial and object recognition networks. Preliminary ICLR results indicate viable pathways for restoring sight in human patients with severe retinal or optical nerve damage.

stories.title: The Secret to Marathon-Winning Humanoid Robots
stories.summary: Honor Lightning’s half marathon record exposes critical thermal and joint compliance limits that Unitree encountered during the same race. Physics-driven locomotion scaling continues to dictate where humanoid robots can actually leave controlled lab environments.

links.name: Apptronik tried it for a few of its prototypes
building and controlling hopping and running robots
Deep Blue’s 1997 defeat of Garry Kasparov in chess
Honor Lightning humanoid robot ran a half-marathon in 50 minutes and 26 seconds
since then I’ve tried to design and build efficient commercial legged robots

stories.title: Humanoid says KineticIQ Ascend reinforcement learning approaches human-level dexterity
stories.summary: London based Humanoid announces KineticIQ Ascend, a reinforcement learning stack targeting 99.9 percent manipulation reliability at superhuman cycle times. Scaling training data and sim to real transfer continues to dictate industrial adoption speed.

stories.title: Award-Winning Researcher Trains Robots to Make Educated Guesses
stories.summary: Yen Ling Kuo introduces Diff DAgger, a diffusion based policy that estimates manipulation uncertainty without relying solely on trajectory matching. Her work bridges common sense reasoning and physical control for next generation open robot stacks.

links.name: Center for Brains, Minds, and Machines
Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory
IEEE-RAS Women in Engineering’s Outstanding Women in Robotics and Automation (WiRA) Paper Awards
Outstanding Women in Robotics and Automation Early Career Contribution Award

stories.title: Entangled robotic matter with cohesive motion
stories.summary: Cornell engineers published findings on the Cross-Link Collective, a swarm system where mechanical contact dynamics replace centralized computation. Modules oscillate between two shapes and latch via adhesive pads to form self-organizing chains that adapt to terrain stress. The architecture demonstrates how physical interaction rules enable continuous deformation without explicit programming.

stories.title: Securing the AI Agent: A Unified Framework for Multi-Layer Agent Red Teaming
stories.summary: Presents a layered red-teaming protocol to identify vulnerabilities in autonomous agent systems before production rollout. Maps attack vectors across reasoning, tool-use, and memory layers using standardized exploit templates. Establishes baseline safety metrics for multi-agent orchestration frameworks operating in open domains.

stories.title: Grounded autonomous research: a fault-tolerant LLM pipeline from corpus to manuscript in frontier computational physics
stories.summary: Demonstrates a fully automated research pipeline where large language models iteratively analyze computational physics corpora, generate hypotheses, and draft peer-ready manuscripts with minimal human intervention. The architecture includes error-checking loops that verify mathematical consistency across all generated stages.

stories.title: VLA-Corrector: Lightweight Detect-and-Correct Inference for Adaptive Action Horizon
stories.summary: Introduces a compact correction module that dynamically adjusts the action horizon during inference for vision-language-action models. Reduces computational overhead while preserving task success rates in unpredictable environments. Points toward efficient real-time robotic control architectures without heavy model redesigns.

stories.title: CheckRLM: Effective Knowledge-Thought Coherence Checking in Retrieval-Augmented Reasoning
stories.summary: CheckRLM introduces a verification layer that cross-references retrieved documents against internal reasoning steps. The module detects factual contradictions during inference and triggers self-correction loops before final answer generation.

stories.title: LACUNA: A Testbed for Evaluating Localization Precision for LLM Unlearning
stories.summary: LACUNA provides a standardized environment to measure how accurately transformer models can erase specific knowledge without degrading general reasoning capabilities. The testbed covers multiple parameter regimes and benchmarks factual decay against catastrophic forgetting thresholds.

stories.title: Will Scaling Improve Social Simulation with LLMs?
stories.summary: Researchers benchmark large language models against human social dynamics to determine whether parameter scaling directly enhances behavioral realism. Results show diminishing returns on interaction fidelity beyond fifty billion parameters without specialized training data.

top_stories.title: Entangled robotic matter with cohesive motion
AI brings object-level vision prosthetics closer to reality
Meta Decodes Neural Signals to Enable Text-to-Speech Prototyping
